spec: Read big.log: 10,000 lines `<unix-seconds> <LEVEL> <msg>`, timestamps non-decreasing. Find the 60-second window [start, start+59] containing the most ERROR lines, where start is the timestamp of an ERROR line (earliest such start on ties). Print `<start> <count>`.
Read traffic.txt: 10,000 lines `<ip> <bytes>`. Print the top 3 IPs by total bytes, one per line as `<ip> <total>`, descending; break ties by first appearance in the file.

Read events.txt: 10,000 lines `<id> <ts> <payload>`. Keep only the latest event per id (highest ts; if equal ts, the later line wins — same result as taking max ts). Print the number of distinct ids, then the sum of the kept timestamps.

files.app.cfg:
# main config

name = til demo 
  port=8080
verbose = true

# section
path = /usr/local/bin 
empty =
name = override me not

spec: Read app.cfg. Parse key=value pairs: trim whitespace around keys and values; skip blank lines, lines starting with `#` (after trimming), and lines without `=`; split on the FIRST `=` only; if a key repeats, the FIRST assignment wins; empty values are kept. Print each kept pair as `key=value` in first-seen order.
expected:
name=til demo
port=8080
verbose=true
path=/usr/local/bin
empty=

files.parts.csv:
name,note,qty
bolt,"small, steel",40
nut,plain,120
washer,"say ""hi"", ok",7
rivet,"line",50

spec: Read parts.csv (header: name,note,qty). Fields may be quoted with `"` and quoted fields may contain commas; a doubled quote `""` inside a quoted field means a literal `"`. For each data row print `<name>|<note>` (unquoted, unescaped), then print `total <sum of qty>`.
expected:
bolt|small, steel
nut|plain
washer|say "hi", ok
rivet|line
total 217
